titleStmt.title: Letter from Julia Evans to Mabel FitzGerald, 13 May 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Julia Evans

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: This is a letter from Julia Evans to Mabel FitzGerald (née Washington McConnell) (1884-1958), wife of politician and intellectual Desmond FitzGerald (1888-1947). Mrs. Evans writes to thank Mabel for her 'kind assistance in our great distress'. Her husband had been arrested after the Rising and she sympathises with Mabel having learned that Mr FitzGerald is also under arrest. Mabel was a former governess in London. She later vigorously canvassed her former acquaintances in London's liberal circles for support for Francis Sheehy Skeffington (1878 -1916) during his hunger strike in 1915. Both Mabel and Desmond would serve in the GPO garrison during Easter Week.

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
22 Harolds Cross Road Dublin May 13.1916
To Mrs.Desmond Fitzgerald Dear Madam I am very thankful
to you for your kindness in
assisting me in my trouble.
I am very sorry to learn
your husband is also under
arrest. God grant they may
soon be released to us.
Hope your children are getting
better and may the Lord comfort
and console you, again
thanking you for your kind
assistance in our great distress Respectfully Yours Julia Evans
